info.name: Attendify - Student API [MASTER]
info.description:
Student workspace endpoints for Attendify.

Frontend auth flow for this collection:
1. `POST /login`
2. Save `response.data.data.token` and `response.data.data.user`
3. `GET /me`
4. `POST /devices/token` if Firebase push is enabled
5. Use the bearer token on all later requests
6. `DELETE /devices/token` before `POST /logout`

Important distinctions: `/resources` is course-specific material for the current student scope, `/library` is the shared study library, `/notifications` is the personal inbox, `/news-hub` is the public communication stream, and `/authorized-grades` is only for users who received delegated grading tasks.

item.name: 01 Section

request.description:
Authenticate a student account.

Request body:
- `login`: email or student_number
- `password`

Save the returned token from `response.data.data.token`, save the user object from `response.data.data.user`, then call `GET /me`.
Use this request immediately after login and on app start to verify the saved student token and hydrate the current profile.

Register or refresh the Firebase device token for the current student session.

Call this immediately after a successful login and again whenever the FCM token changes.
Request body:
- `device_token`: the Firebase/FCM token from the device
- `platform`: `android`, `ios`, or `web`
- `device_name`: optional human-readable device label
- `app_version`: optional app version string

Remove the Firebase device token for the current student session.

Call this before logout when push notifications are enabled, then call the normal logout endpoint and clear local storage.
If push is enabled, call `DELETE /devices/token` first with the current FCM token, then call logout and clear local storage.

item.description:
Authentication runtime for student clients:
1. Login with `login` and `password`
2. Save `response.data.data.token`
3. Call `GET /me`
4. Register Firebase token with `POST /devices/token`
5. Send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on all protected requests
6. Remove the device token before logout

item.name: 03 Section

request.description: Recommended endpoint for the student announcements page. Use `source=all|administration|doctor|delegate` to switch between the combined feed or a specific source.

query.description: Allowed values: all, administration, doctor, delegate

request.description: Major/level announcements only. Use this when you need batch announcements without doctor posts. Administrative and delegate posts share this endpoint and are split by creator role.
Doctor-only subject announcements. Supports the `type` filter in the API to narrow the feed to doctor announcement types.
Personal student inbox notifications, including polls and unread state. This is not the same as the public news feed.
Returns the current unread notifications count for the student inbox.
Cast a vote on a poll notification from the personal inbox.

item.description:
Unified student communication area.

- `news-hub`: the recommended endpoint. It combines official news center items, administrative announcements, doctor announcements, and delegate announcements in one feed with a `source` filter.
- `announcements`: major/level announcements only. In practice this endpoint can contain administrative or delegate posts depending on the creator role.
- `doctor-announcements`: doctor-only subject announcements.
- `notifications`: personal inbox-style notifications for the logged-in student, including polls and unread state.

request.description: Create a flashcard tree node. `display_mode` is now the default type for newly added items, while each item can carry its own `item_type`. Use `parent_pack_id` to nest the new pack under another pack.

body.raw:
{
    "title":  "Clinical Review Root",
    "description":  "Main tree pack",
    "display_mode":  "flash_card",
    "parent_pack_id":  null,
    "repeat_cycle":  "daily",
    "daily_notification_count":  5,
    "notifications_enabled":  true
}

request.description: Returns due items only, sorted by review weight and priority. Each queue entry now includes `item_type`, `item_color`, and `response_actions` for Easy / Medium / Hard notification actions.
Returns a mixed review session for the selected pack tree. Items may be `flash_card`, `one_line`, `qa`, or `mcq` inside the same pack tree.
Use `response_level` instead of the old boolean rating. Valid values: `easy`, `medium`, `hard`.

item.name: 07 Study Center
item.description:
Canonical Study Center endpoints.

- `GET /study-center` returns the personal study hub with normalized items, stats, tabs, and non-duplicated reference summaries.
- `GET /schedules` is different: it returns the official weekly timetable only.
- Legacy `/schedule` endpoints still work, but new clients should use `/study-center`.

request.description: Returns the student's clinical dashboard payload including daily logs, assignments, and form options. Assignment objects now include workflow fields such as `status`, `student_completion_message`, `submitted_at`, `review_notes`, and `reviewed_at`.
Submit a clinical assignment back to the doctor for review. Allowed for assignments in `assigned` or `rejected` status.

Returns the allowed inquiry destinations for the logged-in student. Display each item as doctor name - subject name, and show the inquiry status_label/status_message so the student can block closed subjects before submitting.
Returns the student inquiries list. Inquiry items now include subject, doctor, and responder context for easier display.

Create a new doctor inquiry. First call Inquiry Create Options to present selectable items as doctor name + subject name, then send the chosen subject_id with title and question. If the selected subject is closed, the API returns a clear rejection.
Returns the full inquiry details with subject, related doctor information, and the responder information when the inquiry has been answered.

item.name: Auto Generation Logic (Cards) - Legacy incomplete payload
18 Section

request.description: Delegated grade tasks only. This endpoint is empty for regular students unless a doctor or delegate has explicitly assigned a grading task.
Loads one delegated grade category. The response now distinguishes access_mode=direct or helper, and helper tasks may be full-scope or partial to selected students only.
Stores delegated grades as pending review. Direct and helper delegations are both supported, but helper tasks are restricted to their assigned scope only.

item.description: Authorized grade tasks for students/delegates. This section is used only when a doctor delegates a specific grade category to the current user. It is not available in practice for every student. Workflow: doctor creates grade category -> delegates category -> authorized user enters scores -> doctor reviews pending grades.
